name: fluidsim
description: Framework for computational fluid dynamics simulations using Python. Use when running fluid dynamics simulations including Navier-Stokes equations (2D/3D), shallow water equations, stratified flows, or when analyzing turbulence, vortex dynamics, or geophysical flows. Provides pseudospectral methods with FFT, HPC support, and comprehensive output analysis.
capabilities: Solve 2D and 3D Navier-Stokes equations with pseudospectral methods
Simulate stratified flows and shallow water equations for geophysical applications
Configure viscosity, time stepping, and initial conditions via Python API
Save and analyze physical fields, spatial means, and energy spectra
Run MPI parallel simulations on multiple processors
Perform parametric studies with varying physical parameters
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with instructional content for the FluidSim CFD library. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or command execution capabilities. All static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying markdown code examples and scientific notation as security patterns.
valueStatement: Computational fluid dynamics simulations require complex physics knowledge and specialized software. FluidSim provides a Python framework for solving Navier-Stokes equations with high-performance pseudospectral methods and FFT, enabling accurate turbulence and vortex dynamics research.
userTitle: Run CFD simulations with pseudospectral methods
limitations: Requires Python 3.9 or higher and FFT libraries for pseudospectral methods
Simulations run on periodic domains only (no general boundary conditions)
No built-in visualization for 3D fields beyond HDF5 export to ParaView
MPI installation requires local compilation of mpi4py

useCases.title: Study 2D turbulence cascade
useCases.description: Investigate energy and enstrophy cascades in 2D turbulent flows using spectral analysis

useCases.title: Model stratified ocean flows
useCases.description: Simulate density-stratified flows with Brunt-Väisälä frequency for oceanic applications

useCases.title: Run geophysical flow simulations
useCases.description: Model rotating flows and shallow water dynamics for geophysical and tsunami research

promptTemplates.title: Basic 2D simulation
promptTemplates.scenario: Set up a simple 2D Navier-Stokes simulation
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a 2D Navier-Stokes fluid simulation with FluidSim. Use 256x256 grid points, domain size 2π, viscosity 1e-3, and run for 10 time units with random initial conditions.

promptTemplates.title: Stratified flow
promptTemplates.scenario: Configure stratified flow with density variations
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a stratified flow simulation using ns2d.strat solver. Set Brunt-Väisälä frequency to 2.0 and initialize with a Gaussian density anomaly at the center of the domain.

promptTemplates.title: Parametric study
promptTemplates.scenario: Run multiple simulations with varying viscosity
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a parametric study with FluidSim to investigate how viscosity affects energy decay. Test viscosities of 1e-3, 5e-4, and 1e-4 with 256x256 resolution. Save spatial means and plot final energy vs viscosity.

promptTemplates.title: MPI parallel
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up a high-resolution 3D Navier-Stokes simulation with 512x512x512 grid points using MPI parallelization. Configure the solver to run efficiently on 64 processors with appropriate output periods.

bestPractices: Use powers of 2 for grid resolution (128, 256, 512, 1024) to optimize FFT performance
Enable CFL-adaptive time stepping with CFL=0.5 for stable simulations
Set appropriate output periods to balance data storage and temporal resolution
antiPatterns: Using resolutions not divisible by powers of 2, which degrades FFT performance
Disabling all output types, losing valuable diagnostic information
Running long simulations without checkpointing or field saves

faq.question: Which solver should I use for 3D turbulence?
faq.answer: Use ns3d solver for 3D Navier-Stokes simulations. It supports full 3D turbulence dynamics and MPI parallelization for high-resolution simulations.

faq.question: What is the maximum grid size supported?
faq.answer: Grid size depends on available memory. 512×512×512 typically requires 16-32 GB RAM. Use MPI to distribute larger simulations across multiple processors.

faq.question: How do I restart an interrupted simulation?
faq.answer: Set params.init_fields.type = 'from_file' and specify the path to a saved state file. Set a new t_end to continue the simulation from that point.

faq.question: Is my simulation data safe?
faq.answer: FluidSim saves data locally to the specified output directory. No data is transmitted externally. Set FLUIDSIM_PATH to control where files are saved.

faq.question: Why is my simulation running slowly?
faq.answer: Check your FFT library selection (pyfftw is faster than numpy fft), increase grid resolution to powers of 2, enable MPI for parallel processing, and reduce output frequency.

faq.question: How does FluidSim compare to OpenFOAM?
faq.answer: FluidSim uses pseudospectral methods for higher accuracy on periodic domains, while OpenFOAM uses finite volume for general geometries. FluidSim is ideal for fundamental turbulence research.
